sections.content: The purple square moved again! Let me check its new position and understand the pattern. It seems like clicking on a gray dot along the path moves the purple square to that position. Let me verify and then figure out the most efficient way to complete the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check purple square position after clicking gray dot at (51, 12)
after = current_frame.segmentation
purple_after = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
print("Purple objects:")
for n in purple_after:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check gray dots
gray_after = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"\nGray single pixels: {len(gray_after)}")
for n in sorted(gray_after, key=lambda x: x['boundary'][0][0]):
    print(f"  {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the pattern more clearly:
- The purple square moved from [[52, 9], [52, 11], [54, 11], [54, 9]] to [[50, 11], [50, 13], [52, 13], [52, 11]]
- The gray dot at (51, 12) is gone (replaced by the purple square)
- A new gray dot appeared at (53, 10) where the purple square was before
So the mechanic is: clicking on a gray dot along the path moves the purple square to that position, and the old position becomes a gray dot again.
The path goes from the blue character at the top-right down to the bottom-left. The purple square is moving along this path.
The goal seems to be to move the purple square all the way to the blue character (or to the top purple square?).
Looking at the gray dots, they form a diagonal line. The purple square is currently at approximately (51, 12) and needs to move up along the path.
The remaining gray dots above the purple square are:
(49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32), (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42), (19, 44), (17, 46)
That's 17 gray dots above the purple square. Plus the blue character at the end.
If I need to click on each gray dot to move the purple square one step at a time, that would be 17+ more clicks. But the efficiency budget says "3 of about 22 target actions", which suggests there are about 19 more actions needed. That's close to 17 gray dots + maybe some final action.
But wait - maybe I can click further along the path to skip multiple steps? Let me try clicking on a gray dot further up the path to see if the purple square jumps there.
Actually, looking at the first move, the purple square jumped from the bottom-left corner to the white cross position, which was several dots away. So maybe I can click on any position on the path and the purple square will jump there?
Let me try clicking on a gray dot near the blue character to see if the purple square jumps all the way there.

# Let me try clicking near the blue character to see if it jumps all the way.
# The gray dot closest to the blue character is at (17, 46)

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 46}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
